Fair Political Practices Committee
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Dear John:
As we discussed on the phone last weelg I am interested in receiving some
zuidelines that will help the South Coast Air Qualiw Manaqement District better
Inderstand and interpret the Fair Political Practices Act. I*understand that it has
been amended several times in the past year, and would like to have your written
opinion regarding specific situations we frequently encounter. As background
information, the AOMD is led by a l2-member governing board - nine are elected
officials; three are appointed either by the legislature or governor.
1.
We have several ongoing newsletters that address different audiences,
including employees, thought leaders, and CEOs of major companies. We
have been entertaining the idea of running features on each of our AQMD
Board Members (along with their photos) as a way to educate our readers
about the people who represent their interests. These mailings exceed the
200-person limit and are sent out at public expense. Is this allowable?
When is it permissible to include the names, positions and photos of AQMD
board members on our outreach literature?
Is it permissible to use the name and/or photo of only one or two board
members on a piece of outreach literature (i.e. the chairman and vice-
chairman) if it is not sent our at public expense? If it is? (Again, assuming
the mailing exceeds the 2OGperson limit.)
Any other information you can provide us with regarding guidelines we can use to
determine what is and what is not within the boundries of the FPPC would be
appreciated. If you have any questions, please feel free to call me at (818) 572-6108.
Thank you.
3.
Sincerelyo A

sections.question: 1. We have several ongoing newsletters that address different audiences, including employees, thought leaders, and CEOs of major companies. We have been entertaining the idea of running features on each of our AQMD Board Members (along with their photos) as a way to educate our readers about the people who represent their interests. These mailings exceed the 200-person limit and are sent out at public expense. Is this allowable? When is it permissible to include the names, positions and photos of AQMD board members on our outreach literature? Is it permissible to use the name and/or photo of only one or two board members on a piece of outreach literature (i.e. the chairman and vice-chairman) if it is not sent our at public expense? If it is? (Again, assuming the mailing exceeds the 200-person limit.) Any other information you can provide us with regarding guidelines we can use to determine what is and what is not within the boundries of the FPPC would be appreciated.
sections.question_synthetic: The South Coast Air Quality Management District seeks guidance on whether it may feature AQMD board members' names, positions, and photos in newsletters sent to various audiences, particularly when the mailing exceeds 200 persons and is sent at public expense. They also ask whether using only one or two board members' names/photos is permissible under different funding scenarios.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two separate incoming requests rather than FPPC responses. First request from John Duntap (South Coast AQMD) addresses board member representation in newsletters. Second request from Galen Larson/Betty Vaughan (Fresno County) concerns campaign disclosure and contribution regulations. No FPPC conclusion or advice letters are present in this document. Document is clearly incoming requests to FPPC, not FPPC responses.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: 1. We have several ongoing newsletters that address different audiences, including employees, thought leaders, and CEOs of major companies. We have been entertaining the idea of running features on each of our AQMD Board Members (along with their photos) as a way to educate our readers about the people who represent their interests. These mailings exceed the 200-person limit and are sent out at public expense. Is this allowable? When is it permissible to include the names, positions and photos of AQMD board members on our outreach literature? Is it permissible to use the name and/or photo of only one or two board members on a piece of outreach literature (i.e. the chairman and vice-chairman) if it is not sent our at public expense? If it is? (Again, assuming the mailing exceeds the 200-person limit.) Any other information you can provide us with regarding guidelines we can use to determine what is and what is not within the boundries of the FPPC would be appreciated.

embedding.summary: This document contains two separate incoming requests to the FPPC: one from the South Coast Air Quality Management District (April 17, 1990) asking about use of board member names and photos in public newsletters, and one from Fresno County (May 1, 1990) asking about county ordinance provisions for paying candidate statement printing and mailing costs.
